 `./clients/chess-web/src/components/board.rs`

```text
1	//! SVG board renderer. Variant-agnostic: takes a `PlayerView` + observer +
2	//! a click callback, and lays cells on the intersection grid that the TUI
3	//! also uses (see `crate::orient`).
4	
5	use chess_core::board::BoardShape;
6	use chess_core::coord::Square;
7	use chess_core::piece::Side;
8	use chess_core::view::{PlayerView, VisibleCell};
9	use leptos::*;
10	
11	use crate::glyph::{self, Style};
12	use crate::orient::{display_dims, square_at_display};
13	use crate::state::legal_targets;
14	
15	pub const CELL: i32 = 60;
16	pub const PAD: i32 = 30;
17	const RIVER_TOP_ROW: u8 = 4;
18	const RIVER_BOT_ROW: u8 = 5;
19	const TILE_RADIUS: i32 = 24;
20	
21	#[component]
22	pub fn Board(
23	    shape: BoardShape,
24	    observer: Side,
25	    #[prop(into)] view: Signal<PlayerView>,
26	    #[prop(into)] selected: Signal<Option<Square>>,
27	    #[prop(into)] on_click: Callback<Square>,
28	) -> impl IntoView {
29	    let (rows, cols) = display_dims(shape);
30	    let view_w = (cols as i32 - 1) * CELL + PAD * 2;
31	    let view_h = (rows as i32 - 1) * CELL + PAD * 2;
32	    let viewbox = format!("0 0 {} {}", view_w, view_h);
33	    let style = Style::Cjk;
34	
35	    view! {
36	        <svg class="board" viewBox=viewbox preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet">
37	            <rect class="board-bg" x="0" y="0" width="100%" height="100%"/>
38	            <g class="grid-layer">{grid_lines(shape, rows, cols)}</g>
39	            <g class="river-layer">{river_text(shape, cols)}</g>
40	            <g class="palace-layer">{palace_diagonals(shape)}</g>
41	            <g class="overlay-layer">
42	                {move || selection_marker(selected.get(), observer, shape)}
43	                {move || move_dots_view(&view.get(), selected.get(), observer, shape)}
44	            </g>
45	            <g class="pieces-layer">
46	                {move || pieces_view(&view.get(), observer, shape, style)}
47	            </g>
48	            <g class="cells-layer">
49	                {hit_cells(rows, cols, observer, shape, on_click)}
50	            </g>
51	        </svg>
52	    }
53	}
54	
55	#[inline]
56	fn intersection(row: u8, col: u8) -> (i32, i32) {
57	    (PAD + col as i32 * CELL, PAD + row as i32 * CELL)
58	}
59	
60	fn grid_lines(shape: BoardShape, rows: u8, cols: u8) -> View {
61	    let is_xiangqi = matches!(shape, BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10);
62	    let mut out: Vec<View> = Vec::with_capacity(rows as usize + cols as usize * 2);
63	    for r in 0..rows {
64	        let y = PAD + r as i32 * CELL;
65	        let x1 = PAD;
66	        let x2 = PAD + (cols as i32 - 1) * CELL;
67	        out.push(view! { <line class="grid" x1=x1 y1=y x2=x2 y2=y/> }.into_view());
68	    }
69	    for c in 0..cols {
70	        let x = PAD + c as i32 * CELL;
71	        let is_border = c == 0 || c == cols - 1;
72	        let y_top = PAD;
73	        let y_bot = PAD + (rows as i32 - 1) * CELL;
74	        if is_xiangqi && !is_border {
75	            let y_river_top = PAD + RIVER_TOP_ROW as i32 * CELL;
76	            let y_river_bot = PAD + RIVER_BOT_ROW as i32 * CELL;
77	            out.push(
78	                view! {
79	                    <line class="grid" x1=x y1=y_top x2=x y2=y_river_top/>
80	                    <line class="grid" x1=x y1=y_river_bot x2=x y2=y_bot/>
81	                }
82	                .into_view(),
83	            );
84	        } else {
85	            out.push(view! { <line class="grid" x1=x y1=y_top x2=x y2=y_bot/> }.into_view());
86	        }
87	    }
88	    out.into_view()
89	}
90	
91	fn river_text(shape: BoardShape, cols: u8) -> View {
92	    if !matches!(shape, BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10) {
93	        return ().into_view();
94	    }
95	    let cx = PAD + (cols as i32 - 1) * CELL / 2;
96	    let cy = PAD + (RIVER_TOP_ROW as i32 * CELL + RIVER_BOT_ROW as i32 * CELL) / 2;
97	    view! {
98	        <text class="river-text" x=cx y=cy text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">
99	            "楚 河 漢 界"
100	        </text>
101	    }
102	    .into_view()
103	}
104	
105	fn palace_diagonals(shape: BoardShape) -> View {
106	    if !matches!(shape, BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10) {
107	        return ().into_view();
108	    }
109	    // Files 3..5; top palace = display rows 0..2; bottom palace = rows 7..9.
110	    // Symmetric for both observers because the palace box is centered on the file axis.
111	    let segments = [
112	        (intersection(0, 3), intersection(2, 5)),
113	        (intersection(0, 5), intersection(2, 3)),
114	        (intersection(7, 3), intersection(9, 5)),
115	        (intersection(7, 5), intersection(9, 3)),
116	    ];
117	    segments
118	        .into_iter()
119	        .map(|(a, b)| view! { <line class="palace" x1=a.0 y1=a.1 x2=b.0 y2=b.1/> }.into_view())
120	        .collect::<Vec<View>>()
121	        .into_view()
122	}
123	
124	fn pieces_view(view: &PlayerView, observer: Side, shape: BoardShape, style: Style) -> View {
125	    let (rows, cols) = display_dims(shape);
126	    let mut out: Vec<View> = Vec::new();
127	    for row in 0..rows {
128	        for col in 0..cols {
129	            let Some(sq) = square_at_display(row, col, observer, shape) else { continue };
130	            let cell = view.cells[sq.0 as usize];
131	            let (cx, cy) = intersection(row, col);
132	            match cell {
133	                VisibleCell::Empty => {}
134	                VisibleCell::Hidden => {
135	                    out.push(
136	                        view! {
137	                            <g class="tile hidden" transform=format!("translate({}, {})", cx, cy)>
138	                                <circle class="tile-back" r=TILE_RADIUS cx="0" cy="0"/>
139	                            </g>
140	                        }
141	                        .into_view(),
142	                    );
143	                }
144	                VisibleCell::Revealed(p) => {
145	                    let side_class = match p.piece.side {
146	                        Side::RED => "red",
147	                        Side::BLACK => "black",
148	                        _ => "green",
149	                    };
150	                    out.push(
151	                        view! {
152	                            <g class=format!("tile piece {}", side_class) transform=format!("translate({}, {})", cx, cy)>
153	                                <circle class="tile-disc" r=TILE_RADIUS cx="0" cy="0"/>
154	                                <text class="tile-glyph" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">
155	                                    {glyph::glyph(p.piece.kind, p.piece.side, style)}
156	                                </text>
157	                            </g>
158	                        }
159	                        .into_view(),
160	                    );
161	                }
162	            }
163	        }
164	    }
165	    out.into_view()
166	}
167	
168	fn selection_marker(selected: Option<Square>, observer: Side, shape: BoardShape) -> View {
169	    let Some(sq) = selected else { return ().into_view() };
170	    let (rows, cols) = display_dims(shape);
171	    for row in 0..rows {
172	        for col in 0..cols {
173	            if square_at_display(row, col, observer, shape) == Some(sq) {
174	                let (cx, cy) = intersection(row, col);
175	                return view! { <circle class="selection" cx=cx cy=cy r="28"/> }.into_view();
176	            }
177	        }
178	    }
179	    ().into_view()
180	}
181	
182	fn move_dots_view(
183	    view: &PlayerView,
184	    selected: Option<Square>,
185	    observer: Side,
186	    shape: BoardShape,
187	) -> View {
188	    let Some(from) = selected else { return ().into_view() };
189	    let targets = legal_targets(view, from);
190	    if targets.is_empty() {
191	        return ().into_view();
192	    }
193	    let (rows, cols) = display_dims(shape);
194	    let mut out: Vec<View> = Vec::new();
195	    for row in 0..rows {
196	        for col in 0..cols {
197	            let Some(sq) = square_at_display(row, col, observer, shape) else { continue };
198	            if targets.contains(&sq) {
199	                let (cx, cy) = intersection(row, col);
200	                // Distinct dot for "move to my own square" (banqi reveal) — same
201	                // visual treatment for now; refined in commit 3.
202	                out.push(view! { <circle class="move-dot" cx=cx cy=cy r="9"/> }.into_view());
203	            }
204	        }
205	    }
206	    out.into_view()
207	}
208	
209	fn hit_cells(
210	    rows: u8,
211	    cols: u8,
212	    observer: Side,
213	    shape: BoardShape,
214	    on_click: Callback<Square>,
215	) -> View {
216	    let mut out: Vec<View> = Vec::with_capacity(rows as usize * cols as usize);
217	    for row in 0..rows {
218	        for col in 0..cols {
219	            let Some(sq) = square_at_display(row, col, observer, shape) else { continue };
220	            let x = col as i32 * CELL;
221	            let y = row as i32 * CELL;
222	            out.push(
223	                view! {
224	                    <rect
225	                        class="cell-hit"
226	                        x=x y=y width=CELL height=CELL
227	                        on:click=move |_| on_click.call(sq)
228	                    />
229	                }
230	                .into_view(),
231	            );
232	        }
233	    }
234	    out.into_view()
235	}
236
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 09:31:55Z)**_

You're right and I just spotted a second related bug while looking at this — `chess-web`'s selection check `p.piece.side == v.side_to_move` compares a piece-color against the seat name, which after a first flip in banqi can be mismatched (the BLACK-seat may actually be playing Red-colored pieces). That can also block clicks. Let me fix BOTH issues — the selection check AND the chain-builder UX — in chess-web (and chess-tui).

```text
1242	fn compute_select_outcome(n: &NetView, observer: Side) -> SelectOutcome {
1243	    let Some(view) = n.last_view.as_ref() else {
1244	        return SelectOutcome::Ignore;
1245	    };
1246	    if !matches!(view.status, GameStatus::Ongoing) {
1247	        return SelectOutcome::Msg("Game over.".into());
1248	    }
1249	    let shape = view.shape;
1250	    let Some(sq) = orient::square_at_display(n.cursor.0, n.cursor.1, observer, shape) else {
1251	        return SelectOutcome::Msg("Cursor not on a playable square.".into());
1252	    };
1253	    if view.side_to_move != observer {
1254	        return SelectOutcome::Msg("Not your turn.".into());
1255	    }
1256	    match n.selected {
1257	        None => {
1258	            if view.legal_moves.iter().any(|m| m.origin_square() == sq) {
1259	                SelectOutcome::Select(sq)
1260	            } else if matches!(view.cells[sq.0 as usize], VisibleCell::Hidden) {
1261	                SelectOutcome::Msg("Hidden piece. Press 'f' to flip.".into())
1262	            } else {
1263	                SelectOutcome::Msg("No legal move from that square.".into())
1264	            }
1265	        }
1266	        Some(from) if from == sq => SelectOutcome::Deselect,
1267	        Some(from) => {
1268	            let candidate = view
1269	                .legal_moves
1270	                .iter()
1271	                .find(|m| m.origin_square() == from && m.to_square() == Some(sq))
1272	                .cloned();
1273	            match candidate {
1274	                Some(mv) => SelectOutcome::Commit(mv),
1275	                None => SelectOutcome::ClearAndMsg("Illegal move.".into()),
1276	            }
1277	        }
1278	    }
1279	}

1296	        return;
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 09:41:49Z)**_

## Summary of the chain-builder fix

You're right — I tested the engine but skipped the actual web flow. I've now shipped the chain-builder UX in both clients **plus** fixed a second bug I spotted while wiring it.

**Bug 1 — chain capture didn't work click-by-click in chess-web** ✓
- Engine emits atomic `Move::ChainCapture { from, path }` whose `to_square()` returns the *final* hop. So clicking the first capture target only matched the single `Move::Capture` and committed that.
- New: `clients/chess-web/src/state.rs` adds `ChainBuilder` + `chain_starts_here` / `chain_extends` / `commit_chain` helpers (with a unit test). `LocalPage` and `BoardWrapper` track `chain_builder: RwSignal<Option<ChainBuilder>>` and use it to:
  - When the first capture click would seed a multi-hop chain, **enter chain-builder** instead of committing.
  - Subsequent clicks **extend** the path along the same line.
  - Click on an already-committed hop or on the original piece **commits** at that point.
  - Click anywhere else **cancels** and falls through to a fresh selection.
- Board component takes a new optional `chain_hops: Signal<Vec<Square>>` and renders committed hops with a dashed yellow ring + numbered badge (1, 2, 3…). CSS classes `.chain-hop` and `.chain-hop-num` added in `style.css`.

**Bug 2 — chess-web couldn't select pieces after a banqi first-flip-to-opposite-color** ✓
- The selection check `p.piece.side == v.side_to_move` compared a piece-color against the seat. After my engine fix, those can differ in banqi (BLACK-seat may play Red pieces). Replaced with `v.legal_moves.iter().any(|m| m.origin_square() == sq)`, which lets the engine's `current_color()` filter do the right thing. Applied in both `LocalPage` and `BoardWrapper`.

**chess-tui got the same chain-builder treatment.** Added `chain_builder` field to `GameView` + `NetView`, new `SelectOutcome::{StartChain, ExtendChain, CommitChain, CancelChain}` variants, helpers (`chain_starts_here` / `chain_extends` / `commit_chain` mirroring the web version), and Esc / new-game / undo / apply_move all clear the builder. The TUI uses sidebar messages rather than custom board highlights for the chain status, since the existing legal-target dot set already shows next-hop options.

**Verification:**
- `cargo test --workspace` — 31 chess-web tests (incl. new chain-builder helper round-trip), all engine tests, all chess-tui tests pass.
- `cargo fmt --check` clean. `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean.
- `cargo build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p chess-core` clean.
- `trunk build --release` produces the bundle without warnings.

To verify in your browser: rebuild and reload `/local/banqi?house=chain,dark,rush,horse&seed=...`, select a piece that has a chain available (e.g. Red horse with two black soldiers in line), click the first soldier — you should see a yellow dashed ring with `1` on it instead of an immediate capture. Click the next square in line to extend (ring `2`), then click the same hop or the original piece to commit the full chain.

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 10:01:59Z)**_

You're right — this is the deeper bug. My chain-builder UX was UI-only state, but the user's actual semantic requires the **engine** to not auto-advance the turn after a chain-eligible capture. Currently the engine commits the atomic `Move::Capture` and advances the turn, regardless of whether more captures are available. The chain-builder I added only worked for same-line `Move::ChainCapture` commits — it didn't help when the next capture is in a different direction (your `相` capturing `砲` east, then wanting to capture `卒` south).

The right fix is engine-driven chain mode: a `chain_lock` state + `Move::EndChain`. Let me overhaul this properly.

```text
15	///
16	/// `ChainCapture` is a single move even though it encodes a sequence —
17	/// undo pops once, history records once, the network ships one message.
18	#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
19	pub enum Move {
20	    /// Banqi: flip the face-down piece at `at`. The identity is `None`
21	    /// when the client requests the flip and `Some(_)` once the
22	    /// authoritative engine has resolved it.
23	    Reveal { at: Square, revealed: Option<Piece> },
24	
25	    /// Plain non-capturing slide.
26	    Step { from: Square, to: Square },
27	
28	    /// Capture (single hop). `captured` is recorded so undo can restore.
29	    Capture { from: Square, to: Square, captured: Piece },
30	
31	    /// Chain capture (banqi house rule 連吃 / 暗連). `path` is the sequence
32	    /// of hops, each of which is a capture. Last hop's `to` is the final
33	    /// landing square. Length ≥ 1.
34	    ChainCapture { from: Square, path: SmallVec<[ChainHop; 4]> },
35	
36	    /// Cannon capture-by-jump. `screen` is the piece jumped over.
37	    CannonJump { from: Square, to: Square, screen: Square, captured: Piece },
38	
39	    /// Banqi house rule `暗吃` (DARK_CAPTURE): atomically reveal a
40	    /// face-down piece and resolve a capture against it.
41	    /// `revealed` and `attacker` are `None` on the wire (client → server)
42	    /// and `Some(_)` once the authoritative engine has filled them in.
43	    /// Outcome (capture / probe / trade) is decided at apply-time from
44	    /// banqi rank rules + `DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE` flag.
45	    DarkCapture { from: Square, to: Square, revealed: Option<Piece>, attacker: Option<Piece> },
46	}
47	
48	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
49	pub struct ChainHop {
50	    pub to: Square,
51	    pub captured: Piece,
52	}
53	
54	/// Move-list type alias — sized to avoid heap allocation in typical positions.
55	pub type MoveList = SmallVec<[Move; 32]>;
56	
57	impl Move {
58	    /// Origin square of the move (where the moving piece started).
59	    /// `Reveal` returns the flip square.
60	    #[inline]
61	    pub fn origin_square(&self) -> Square {
62	        match self {
63	            Move::Reveal { at, .. } => *at,
64	            Move::Step { from, .. }
65	            | Move::Capture { from, .. }
66	            | Move::ChainCapture { from, .. }
67	            | Move::CannonJump { from, .. }
68	            | Move::DarkCapture { from, .. } => *from,
69	        }
70	    }
71	
72	    /// Destination square (where the moving piece ended). `Reveal` has none.
73	    #[inline]
74	    pub fn to_square(&self) -> Option<Square> {
75	        match self {
76	            Move::Reveal { .. } => None,
77	            Move::Step { to, .. }
78	            | Move::Capture { to, .. }
79	            | Move::CannonJump { to, .. }
80	            | Move::DarkCapture { to, .. } => Some(*to),
81	            Move::ChainCapture { path, .. } => path.last().map(|h| h.to),
82	        }
83	    }
84	
85	    /// Whether this move resets the no-progress counter (capture or reveal).
86	    #[inline]
87	    pub fn resets_no_progress(&self) -> bool {
88	        matches!(
89	            self,
90	            Move::Reveal { .. }
91	                | Move::Capture { .. }
92	                | Move::ChainCapture { .. }
93	                | Move::CannonJump { .. }
94	                | Move::DarkCapture { .. }
95	        )
96	    }
97	}
98
```

```text
50	pub struct SideAssignment {
51	    /// `mapping[side.0 as usize]` = the piece-color that seat controls.
52	    pub mapping: SmallVec<[Side; 3]>,
53	}
54	
55	#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
56	pub struct GameState {
57	    pub rules: RuleSet,
58	    pub board: Board,
59	    pub side_to_move: Side,
60	    pub turn_order: TurnOrder,
61	    pub history: Vec<MoveRecord>,
62	    pub status: GameStatus,
63	    /// Banqi only: established after first flip. `None` until then.
64	    pub side_assignment: Option<SideAssignment>,
65	    pub no_progress_plies: u16,
66	}
67	
68	impl GameState {
69	    pub fn new(rules: RuleSet) -> Self {
70	        crate::setup::build_initial_state(rules)
71	    }
72	
73	    /// Generate all legal moves for the side to move.
74	    pub fn legal_moves(&self) -> MoveList {
75	        let mut out = MoveList::new();
76	        crate::rules::generate_moves(self, &mut out);
77	        out
78	    }
79	
80	    /// Apply a move. Caller is responsible for legality (use `legal_moves`).
81	    /// Returns the move pushed to history (with `Reveal` payloads filled in).
82	    pub fn make_move(&mut self, m: &Move) -> Result<(), CoreError> {
83	        if matches!(self.status, GameStatus::Won { .. } | GameStatus::Drawn { .. }) {
84	            return Err(CoreError::GameOver);
85	        }
86	        let mover = self.side_to_move;
87	        let no_progress_before = self.no_progress_plies;
88	
89	        // Normalize the move (fill in Reveal payload from board if missing).
90	        let normalized = self.normalize_for_apply(m)?;
91	        self.apply_inner(&normalized)?;
92	
93	        self.history.push(MoveRecord { mover, the_move: normalized.clone(), no_progress_before });
94	
95	        if normalized.resets_no_progress() {
96	            self.no_progress_plies = 0;
97	        } else {
98	            self.no_progress_plies = self.no_progress_plies.saturating_add(1);
99	        }
100	
101	        self.turn_order.advance();
102	        self.side_to_move = self.turn_order.current_side();
103	
104	        Ok(())
105	    }
106	
107	    /// Undo the last move.
108	    pub fn unmake_move(&mut self) -> Result<(), CoreError> {
109	        let rec = self.history.pop().ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("no move to undo"))?;
110	        self.unapply_inner(&rec.the_move)?;
111	        self.no_progress_plies = rec.no_progress_before;
112	        // Rewind turn order
113	        if self.turn_order.current == 0 {
114	            self.turn_order.current = (self.turn_order.seats.len() as u8) - 1;
115	        } else {
116	            self.turn_order.current -= 1;
117	        }
118	        self.side_to_move = self.turn_order.current_side();
119	        // Game status reset to Ongoing — caller may recompute.
120	        self.status = GameStatus::Ongoing;
121	        Ok(())
122	    }
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
38	/// Place the 32 standard xiangqi pieces. Files indexed 0..9 left-to-right

40	fn place_xiangqi_initial(board: &mut Board) {
41	    use crate::coord::{File, Rank};
42	
43	    let mut back = |kind: PieceKind, side: Side, files: &[u8], rank: u8| {
44	        for &f in files {
45	            board.set(
46	                board.sq(File(f), Rank(rank)),
47	                Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(side, kind))),
48	            );
49	        }
50	    };

52	    // Red back rank (rank 0)

54	    back(PieceKind::Horse, Side::RED, &[1, 7], 0);
55	    back(PieceKind::Elephant, Side::RED, &[2, 6], 0);
56	    back(PieceKind::Advisor, Side::RED, &[3, 5], 0);
57	    back(PieceKind::General, Side::RED, &[4], 0);
58	
59	    // Red cannons (rank 2)
60	    back(PieceKind::Cannon, Side::RED, &[1, 7], 2);
61	
62	    // Red soldiers (rank 3)
63	    back(PieceKind::Soldier, Side::RED, &[0, 2, 4, 6, 8], 3);
64	
65	    // Black mirrors at rank 9, 7, 6

75	fn build_banqi(rules: RuleSet) -> GameState {
76	    let mut board = Board::new(BoardShape::Banqi4x8);
77	    let seed = rules.banqi_seed.unwrap_or_else(|| {
78	        // Nondeterministic in the absence of a seed.
79	        use rand::RngCore;
80	        rand::thread_rng().next_u64()
81	    });
82	    let mut rng = ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(seed);
83	    let mut pieces = banqi_piece_set();
84	    pieces.shuffle(&mut rng);
85	    for (i, p) in pieces.into_iter().enumerate() {
86	        board.set(crate::coord::Square(i as u16), Some(PieceOnSquare::hidden(p)));
87	    }
88	    GameState {
89	        rules,
90	        board,
91	        side_to_move: Side::RED,
92	        turn_order: TurnOrder::two_player(),
93	        history: Vec::new(),
94	        status: GameStatus::Ongoing,
95	        side_assignment: None,
96	        no_progress_plies: 0,
97	    }
98	}
99	
100	/// One xiangqi piece-set per side, all 32 pieces. Order doesn't matter
101	/// because the caller shuffles.
102	fn banqi_piece_set() -> Vec<Piece> {
103	    let mut v = Vec::with_capacity(32);
104	    for side in [Side::RED, Side::BLACK] {
105	        v.push(Piece::new(side, PieceKind::General));
106	        v.extend((0..2).map(|_| Piece::new(side, PieceKind::Advisor)));
107	        v.extend((0..2).map(|_| Piece::new(side, PieceKind::Elephant)));
108	        v.extend((0..2).map(|_| Piece::new(side, PieceKind::Chariot)));
109	        v.extend((0..2).map(|_| Piece::new(side, PieceKind::Horse)));
110	        v.extend((0..2).map(|_| Piece::new(side, PieceKind::Cannon)));
111	        v.extend((0..5).map(|_| Piece::new(side, PieceKind::Soldier)));
112	    }
113	    debug_assert_eq!(v.len(), 32);
114	    v
115	}
```

```text
49	/// Encode a move as ICCS. `Reveal` becomes `flip <sq>`. `ChainCapture`
50	/// chains squares with `x` separators. `DarkCapture` uses `x?` to mark
51	/// the hidden target (e.g. `b2x?b3`).
52	pub fn encode_move(board: &Board, m: &Move) -> String {
53	    match m {
54	        Move::Reveal { at, .. } => format!("flip {}", encode_square(board, *at)),
55	        Move::Step { from, to } => {
56	            format!("{}{}", encode_square(board, *from), encode_square(board, *to))
57	        }
58	        Move::Capture { from, to, .. } | Move::CannonJump { from, to, .. } => {
59	            format!("{}x{}", encode_square(board, *from), encode_square(board, *to))
60	        }
61	        Move::DarkCapture { from, to, .. } => {
62	            format!("{}x?{}", encode_square(board, *from), encode_square(board, *to))
63	        }
64	        Move::ChainCapture { from, path } => {
65	            let mut s = encode_square(board, *from);
66	            for hop in path {
67	                s.push('x');
68	                s.push_str(&encode_square(board, hop.to));
69	            }
70	            s
71	        }
72	    }
73	}
```

```rs
//! 連吃 chain mode (engine-state, not just atomic): after a chain-eligible
//! capture, `chain_lock` activates and the turn does NOT auto-advance until
//! the player either continues capturing in any direction or issues
//! `Move::EndChain`.

use chess_core::board::Board;
use chess_core::coord::{File, Rank, Square};
use chess_core::moves::Move;
use chess_core::piece::{Piece, PieceKind, PieceOnSquare, Side};
use chess_core::rules::{HouseRules, RuleSet};
use chess_core::state::{GameState, SideAssignment};
use smallvec::smallvec;

fn empty_banqi(rules: RuleSet) -> GameState {
    let mut state = GameState::new(rules);
    let squares: Vec<Square> = state.board.squares().collect();
    for sq in squares {
        state.board.set(sq, None);
    }
    state.side_assignment = Some(SideAssignment { mapping: smallvec![Side::RED, Side::BLACK] });
    state
}

fn place(board: &mut Board, sq: Square, side: Side, kind: PieceKind) {
    board.set(sq, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(side, kind))));
}

#[test]
fn capture_into_chain_keeps_turn_with_same_player() {
    // Red horse at a1 captures a2 (black soldier), and from a2 can keep
    // capturing a3 (another black soldier) — chain mode activates.
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, 0));
    let h = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(1));
    let s1 = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(2));
    let s2 = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(3));
    place(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
    place(&mut state.board, s1, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
    place(&mut state.board, s2, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);

    let single = Move::Capture {
        from: h,
        to: s1,
        captured: Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier),
    };
    state.make_move(&single).unwrap();

    assert_eq!(state.chain_lock, Some(s1), "chain_lock activates on capturing piece's new square");
    assert_eq!(state.side_to_move, Side::RED, "turn does NOT advance — still RED");
    assert_eq!(state.current_color(), Side::RED);
}

#[test]
fn chain_legal_moves_filter_to_captures_from_locked_square_plus_end_chain() {
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, 0));
    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
    let s1 = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
    let s2 = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(3));
    let s_side = state.board.sq(File(2), Rank(2));
    let other = state.board.sq(File(3), Rank(0));
    place(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
    place(&mut state.board, s1, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
    place(&mut state.board, s2, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
    place(&mut state.board, s_side, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
    place(&mut state.board, other, Side::RED, PieceKind::Chariot);

    let cap = Move::Capture {
        from: h,
        to: s1,
        captured: Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier),
    };
    state.make_move(&cap).unwrap();

    let legal = state.legal_moves();
    // Only moves originating at s1 (the chain-locked square) + EndChain.
    for m in legal.iter() {
        match m {
            Move::EndChain { at } => assert_eq!(*at, s1),
            other => assert_eq!(other.origin_square(), s1, "all locked legal moves must start at s1"),
        }
    }
    // Specifically, the other Red chariot at `other` must NOT be movable.
    assert!(
        !legal.iter().any(|m| m.origin_square() == other && !matches!(m, Move::EndChain { .. })),
        "non-locked piece must not be in legal_moves"
    );
    // EndChain must always be available.
    assert!(legal.iter().any(|m| matches!(m, Move::EndChain { .. })));
    // Captures in the SAME line (s2) AND in a perpendicular direction
    // (s_side) should both be legal.
    assert!(legal
        .iter()
        .any(|m| matches!(m, Move::Capture { to, .. } if *to == s2)));
    assert!(legal
        .iter()
        .any(|m| matches!(m, Move::Capture { to, .. } if *to == s_side)));
}

#[test]
fn end_chain_advances_turn_and_clears_lock() {
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, 0));
    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
    let s1 = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
    let s2 = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(3));
    place(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
    place(&mut state.board, s1, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
    place(&mut state.board, s2, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);

    let cap = Move::Capture {
        from: h,
        to: s1,
        captured: Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier),
    };
    state.make_move(&cap).unwrap();
    assert!(state.chain_lock.is_some());

    state.make_move(&Move::EndChain { at: s1 }).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(state.chain_lock, None, "EndChain clears the lock");
    assert_eq!(state.side_to_move, Side::BLACK, "turn advances after EndChain");
}

#[test]
fn capture_with_no_followup_advances_turn_normally() {
    // Red horse at a1 captures a2, but no further captures available from a2.
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, 0));
    let h = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(1));
    let s1 = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(2));
    place(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
    place(&mut state.board, s1, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);

    let cap = Move::Capture {
        from: h,
        to: s1,
        captured: Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier),
    };
    state.make_move(&cap).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(state.chain_lock, None, "no further captures from a2 → no chain lock");
    assert_eq!(state.side_to_move, Side::BLACK, "turn advanced normally");
}

#[test]
fn chain_mode_does_not_activate_without_chain_capture_flag() {
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 0));
    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
    let s1 = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
    let s2 = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(3));
    place(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
    place(&mut state.board, s1, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
    place(&mut state.board, s2, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);

    let cap = Move::Capture {
        from: h,
        to: s1,
        captured: Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier),
    };
    state.make_move(&cap).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(state.chain_lock, None, "no chain mode without CHAIN_CAPTURE flag");
    assert_eq!(state.side_to_move, Side::BLACK, "turn advanced normally");
}

#[test]
fn perpendicular_capture_in_chain_keeps_lock() {
    // The user's image scenario: 相 captures 砲 east, then can capture
    // 卒 south in chain mode.
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, 0));
    let elephant = state.board.sq(File(0), Rank(2));
    let cannon_target = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
    let south_soldier = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
    place(&mut state.board, elephant, Side::RED, PieceKind::Elephant);
    place(&mut state.board, cannon_target, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Cannon);
    place(&mut state.board, south_soldier, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);

    let cap1 = Move::Capture {
        from: elephant,
        to: cannon_target,
        captured: Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Cannon),
    };
    state.make_move(&cap1).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(state.chain_lock, Some(cannon_target));
    assert_eq!(state.side_to_move, Side::RED, "still red after first capture");

    // Now from cannon_target capture the south soldier — perpendicular
    // direction, but legal because rules permit any-direction capture
    // when chain_lock is active and the moving piece can capture there.
    let legal = state.legal_moves();
    let cap2 = legal
        .iter()
        .find(|m| matches!(m, Move::Capture { to, .. } if *to == south_soldier))
        .cloned()
        .expect("south soldier capture must be legal");
    state.make_move(&cap2).unwrap();

    // Lock either continues (more captures) or ends (none more) — here
    // there's nothing else, so it should end.
    assert_eq!(state.chain_lock, None);
    assert_eq!(state.side_to_move, Side::BLACK, "turn finally advances when chain dries up");
}

#[test]
fn chain_make_unmake_round_trip() {
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, 0));
    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
    let s1 = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
    let s2 = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(3));
    place(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
    place(&mut state.board, s1, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
    place(&mut state.board, s2, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
    let snapshot = state.clone();

    let cap = Move::Capture {
        from: h,
        to: s1,
        captured: Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier),
    };
    state.make_move(&cap).unwrap();
    assert!(state.chain_lock.is_some());

    state.unmake_move().unwrap();
    assert_eq!(state.board, snapshot.board);
    assert_eq!(state.side_to_move, snapshot.side_to_move);
    assert_eq!(state.chain_lock, snapshot.chain_lock);
}

#[test]
fn end_chain_make_unmake_round_trip() {
    let mut state = empty_banqi(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, 0));
    let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
    let s1 = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
    let s2 = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(3));
    place(&mut state.board, h, Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse);
    place(&mut state.board, s1, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);
    place(&mut state.board, s2, Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier);

    let cap = Move::Capture {
        from: h,
        to: s1,
        captured: Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier),
    };
    state.make_move(&cap).unwrap();
    let snapshot_after_cap = state.clone();

    state.make_move(&Move::EndChain { at: s1 }).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(state.chain_lock, None);
    assert_eq!(state.side_to_move, Side::BLACK);

    state.unmake_move().unwrap();
    assert_eq!(state.board, snapshot_after_cap.board);
    assert_eq!(state.chain_lock, snapshot_after_cap.chain_lock);
    assert_eq!(state.side_to_move, snapshot_after_cap.side_to_move);
}
```

```text
        chain_builder.get().map(|b| b.hops).unwrap_or_default()
    });

    let on_click: Callback<Square> = Callback::new(move |sq: Square| {
        if is_spectator {
            return;
        }
        let v = view.get();
        if v.observer != v.side_to_move {
            return;
        }

        // Chain-builder: extend / commit / cancel.
        if let Some(builder) = chain_builder.get() {
            if let Some(idx) = builder.hops.iter().position(|s| *s == sq) {
                let truncated =
                    ChainBuilder { from: builder.from, hops: builder.hops[..=idx].to_vec() };
                if let Some(mv) = commit_chain(&v, &truncated) {
                    handle.send(ClientMsg::Move { mv });
                    chain_builder.set(None);
                    selected.set(None);
                }
                return;
            }
            if chain_extends(&v, &builder, sq) {
                let mut new_hops = builder.hops.clone();
                new_hops.push(sq);
                chain_builder.set(Some(ChainBuilder { from: builder.from, hops: new_hops }));
                return;
            }
            if sq == builder.from {
                if let Some(mv) = commit_chain(&v, &builder) {
                    handle.send(ClientMsg::Move { mv });
                }
                chain_builder.set(None);
                selected.set(None);
                return;
            }
            chain_builder.set(None);
        }

        let cur = selected.get();
        if cur == Some(sq) {
            selected.set(None);
            return;
        }
        if let Some(from) = cur {
            if let Some(mv) = find_move(&v, from, sq) {
                if chain_starts_here(&v, from, sq) {
                    chain_builder.set(Some(ChainBuilder { from, hops: vec![sq] }));
                    return;
                }
                handle.send(ClientMsg::Move { mv });
                selected.set(None);
                return;
            }
        }
        let cell = v.cells[sq.0 as usize];
        let is_selectable_revealed = matches!(cell, VisibleCell::Revealed(_))
            && v.legal_moves.iter().any(|m| m.origin_square() == sq);
        if is_selectable_revealed {
            selected.set(Some(sq));
        } else if matches!(cell, VisibleCell::Hidden) {
            if let Some(mv) = find_move(&v, sq, sq) {
                handle.send(ClientMsg::Move { mv });
                selected.set(None);
            }
        } else {
            selected.set(None);
        }
    });

    view! {
        <Board
            shape=shape
            observer=obs
            view=view
            selected=selected
            on_click=on_click
            chain_hops=chain_hops_signal
        />
    }
}
```

```text
String:     fn handle_select_or_commit(g: &mut GameView, observer: Side) {
        if !matches!(g.state.status, chess_core::state::GameStatus::Ongoing) {
            g.last_msg =
                Some("Game over. Press 'n' for new game, 'u' to take back, 'q' to quit.".into());
            return;
        }
        let shape = g.state.board.shape();
        let Some(sq) = orient::square_at_display(g.cursor.0, g.cursor.1, observer, shape) else {
            g.last_msg = Some("Cursor not on a playable square.".into());
            return;
        };

        let view = PlayerView::project(&g.state, g.state.side_to_move);

        // Chain-builder mode: extend / commit / cancel.
        if let Some(builder) = g.chain_builder.clone() {
            // Click on an already-committed hop → commit at that point.
            if let Some(idx) = builder.hops.iter().position(|s| *s == sq) {
                let truncated =
                    ChainBuilder { from: builder.from, hops: builder.hops[..=idx].to_vec() };
                if let Some(mv) = commit_chain(&view, &truncated) {
                    g.chain_builder = None;
                    Self::apply_move(g, mv);
                }
                return;
            }
            if chain_extends(&view, &builder, sq) {
                let mut new_hops = builder.hops.clone();
                new_hops.push(sq);
                g.chain_builder = Some(ChainBuilder { from: builder.from, hops: new_hops });
                g.last_msg = Some(format!(
                    "Chain: {} hops. Enter on the last hop or {} to commit; Esc cancels.",
                    g.chain_builder.as_ref().unwrap().hops.len(),
                    encode_square_safe(&g.state, builder.from),
                ));
                return;
            }
            if sq == builder.from {
                if let Some(mv) = commit_chain(&view, &builder) {
                    g.chain_builder = None;
                    Self::apply_move(g, mv);
                    return;
                }
                g.chain_builder = None;
                return;
            }
            // Click anywhere else → cancel chain-builder; fall through.
            g.chain_builder = None;
        }

        match g.selected {
            None => {
                // Try to select a piece. Allowed if there's a legal move from this square.
                let any = view.legal_moves.iter().any(|m| m.origin_square() == sq);
                if any {
                    g.selected = Some(sq);
                    g.last_msg = None;
                } else {
                    // Maybe it's a banqi hidden cell — suggest 'f' to flip.
                    match g.state.board.get(sq) {
                        Some(p) if !p.revealed => {
                            g.last_msg = Some("Hidden piece. Press 'f' to flip.".into());
                        }
                        _ => g.last_msg = Some("No legal move from that square.".into()),
                    }
                }
            }
            Some(from) if from == sq => {
                g.selected = None;
                g.last_msg = None;
            }
            Some(from) => {
                // Find a legal move from `from` to `sq`. Prefer Capture / CannonJump
                // over a chain ending at sq (for banqi); take the first match.
                let candidate = view
                    .legal_moves
                    .iter()
                    .find(|m| m.origin_square() == from && m.to_square() == Some(sq))
                    .cloned();
                match candidate {
                    Some(m) => {
                        // If a longer chain starts with this hop, enter
                        // chain-builder so the user can extend step-by-step.
                        if chain_starts_here(&view, from, sq) {
                            g.chain_builder =
                                Some(ChainBuilder { from, hops: vec![sq] });
                            g.last_msg = Some(
                                "連吃 chain started — Enter to extend, Esc to cancel, click \
                                 the same hop to commit."
                                    .into(),
                            );
                        } else {
                            Self::apply_move(g, m);
                        }
                    }
                    None => {
                        g.last_msg = Some("Illegal move.".into());
                        g.selected = None;
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 10:19:57Z)**_

Good catch — that's a real bug in the engine logic. When a `Move::DarkCapture` resolves to a Probe (revealed piece was your own color, or outranked you and no trade flag) or Trade (attacker dies), the attacker DIDN'T land on `to`. But my `compute_chain_lock_after` was using `to` as the landing square unconditionally, so it was setting `chain_lock` on the just-revealed *own piece* (the 帥 / 仕 in your screenshots) — which then let the user move that piece in chain mode. Fix: for `Move::DarkCapture`, consult `dark_capture_outcome` and only treat the move as chain-eligible when it actually resolved to `Capture` (attacker landed).

```text
1269	enum SelectOutcome {
1270	    Ignore,

1273	    Select(Square),
1274	    Deselect,
1275	    Commit(Move),
1276	    /// Enter chain-builder with this initial hop (after the first capture).
1277	    StartChain {
1278	        from: Square,
1279	        to: Square,
1280	    },
1281	    /// Append `to` to the in-progress chain.
1282	    ExtendChain {
1283	        to: Square,
1284	    },
1285	    /// Commit the in-progress chain — engine receives the matching atomic
1286	    /// `Move::ChainCapture` (or `Move::Capture` if length 1).

1292	// ---- Chain-builder helpers (連吃) ------------------------------------------
1293	//
1294	// Mirror of the chess-web equivalents in `clients/chess-web/src/state.rs`.
1295	// The engine emits atomic `Move::ChainCapture { path }`; the UI tracks a
1296	// `ChainBuilder` of capture-target squares the user has clicked through and
1297	// finds the matching atomic move at commit time. If you edit one copy,
1298	// edit the other (see `backlog/promote-client-shared.md`).
1299	
1300	/// True if some legal `Move::ChainCapture` (length ≥ 2) starts with the hop
1301	/// `from → to`.
1302	fn chain_starts_here(view: &PlayerView, from: Square, to: Square) -> bool {
1303	    view.legal_moves.iter().any(|m| match m {
1304	        Move::ChainCapture { from: f, path } => {
1305	            *f == from && path.first().map(|h| h.to) == Some(to) && path.len() >= 2
1306	        }
1307	        _ => false,
1308	    })
1309	}
1310	
1311	/// True if appending `next` to `builder.hops` still matches the prefix of
1312	/// some legal `Move::ChainCapture`.
1313	fn chain_extends(view: &PlayerView, builder: &ChainBuilder, next: Square) -> bool {
1314	    let n = builder.hops.len();
1315	    view.legal_moves.iter().any(|m| match m {
1316	        Move::ChainCapture { from: f, path } => {
1317	            *f == builder.from
1318	                && path.len() > n
1319	                && path[..n].iter().zip(builder.hops.iter()).all(|(h, s)| h.to == *s)
1320	                && path[n].to == next
1321	        }
1322	        _ => false,
1323	    })
1324	}
1325	
1326	/// Find the `Move` matching the current builder path verbatim — a single
1327	/// `Move::Capture` / `Move::DarkCapture` when `builder.hops.len() == 1`,
1328	/// otherwise the `Move::ChainCapture` whose `path` matches in order.
1329	fn commit_chain(view: &PlayerView, builder: &ChainBuilder) -> Option<Move> {
1330	    if builder.hops.is_empty() {
1331	        return None;
1332	    }
1333	    if builder.hops.len() == 1 {
1334	        let to = builder.hops[0];
1335	        view.legal_moves
1336	            .iter()
1337	            .find(|m| match m {
1338	                Move::Capture { from: f, to: t, .. } => *f == builder.from && *t == to,
1339	                Move::DarkCapture { from: f, to: t, .. } => *f == builder.from && *t == to,
1340	                _ => false,
1341	            })
1342	            .cloned()
1343	    } else {
1344	        view.legal_moves
1345	            .iter()
1346	            .find(|m| match m {
1347	                Move::ChainCapture { from: f, path } => {
1348	                    *f == builder.from
1349	                        && path.len() == builder.hops.len()
1350	                        && path.iter().zip(builder.hops.iter()).all(|(h, s)| h.to == *s)
1351	                }
1352	                _ => false,
1353	            })
1354	            .cloned()
1355	    }
1356	}
1357	
1358	/// Best-effort ICCS encode of a square — returns `"?"` if the board can't
1359	/// be queried. Used only for chain-builder status messages.
1360	fn encode_square_safe(state: &chess_core::state::GameState, sq: Square) -> String {
1361	    chess_core::notation::iccs::encode_square(&state.board, sq)
1362	}
1363	
1364	fn compute_select_outcome(n: &NetView, observer: Side) -> SelectOutcome {
1365	    let Some(view) = n.last_view.as_ref() else {
1366	        return SelectOutcome::Ignore;
1367	    };
1368	    if !matches!(view.status, GameStatus::Ongoing) {
1369	        return SelectOutcome::Msg("Game over.".into());
1370	    }
1371	    let shape = view.shape;
1372	    let Some(sq) = orient::square_at_display(n.cursor.0, n.cursor.1, observer, shape) else {
1373	        return SelectOutcome::Msg("Cursor not on a playable square.".into());
1374	    };
1375	    if view.side_to_move != observer {
1376	        return SelectOutcome::Msg("Not your turn.".into());
1377	    }
1378	
1379	    // Chain-builder mode: extend / commit / cancel.
1380	    if let Some(builder) = n.chain_builder.as_ref() {
1381	        if builder.hops.contains(&sq) {
1382	            return SelectOutcome::CommitChain;
1383	        }
1384	        if chain_extends(view, builder, sq) {
```

```text
1524	            n.selected = None;
1525	            n.last_msg = None;
1526	        }

1529	            n.selected = None;

1531	            n.last_msg = Some("Sent.".into());
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 10:26:06Z)**_

Web bundle rebuilt. Reload `/local/banqi?house=chain,dark,rush,horse&seed=...` to verify.

## Summary of the chain-mode overhaul + the just-fixed regression

**What was wrong before**
- My first chain-fix did atomic `Move::ChainCapture` + UI chain_builder — it only worked for chains in a *single direction*, so 相 east-capturing 砲 then wanting to capture 卒 *south* still ended the turn.
- After the engine-driven chain-mode rewrite, a second bug surfaced: `Move::DarkCapture` resolving to **Probe** (revealed piece is your own colour) or **Trade** (your attacker dies) was wrongly setting `chain_lock` onto whatever piece happened to be at `to` after the reveal — that's the just-revealed friend, not the attacker. So the engine let the user move the freshly-flipped 帥 / 仕 in chain mode.

**What's fixed now**
- Engine state: `state.chain_lock: Option<Square>` + `Move::EndChain { at }`. After any chain-eligible capture the turn does NOT auto-advance; `legal_moves` filters to "captures from the locked square + EndChain"; the player ends the chain by clicking the locked piece (Esc in TUI).
- `compute_chain_lock_after` now consults `dark_capture_outcome` for `Move::DarkCapture` and only treats it as chain-eligible when it actually resolved to `Capture` (attacker landed at `to`). Probe and Trade outcomes correctly skip chain mode and advance the turn.
- New regression tests covering both bugs (`banqi_chain_mode.rs` — 11 tests, all passing): probe-against-own-piece, trade-with-attacker-death, perpendicular capture in chain (the original 相→砲→卒 scenario), make/unmake round-trips for both `Capture` and `EndChain`.

**UI changes**
- Removed the now-redundant UI-side `ChainBuilder` from chess-web (`local.rs`, `play.rs`, `state.rs`) and chess-tui (`GameView` / `NetView` / `SelectOutcome`). The single source of truth is `view.chain_lock` from the projected `PlayerView`.
- chess-web shows a "連吃 — 繼續吃 or [End chain]" banner under the board when `chain_lock` is set. The locked piece renders with a dashed orange ring (the highlight you saw in the latest screenshot).
- chess-tui: in chain mode pressing Enter on the locked square sends `Move::EndChain`; pressing Enter elsewhere either captures (if legal from the locked square) or shows a guidance message. Esc now ends the chain when chain mode is active (instead of clearing a phantom UI builder).
- Fixed the chess-web piece-selection check (`p.piece.side == v.side_to_move` → `legal_moves.iter().any(...)`) so banqi after a first-flip-to-opposite-colour properly lets the seat select its own pieces.

**Verification:** all 248 workspace tests pass, fmt & clippy `-D warnings` clean, wasm32 build clean, `trunk build --release` clean.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 10:30:41Z)**_

Right — that's the seat name leaking through to the user-facing label. After Player 1 (RED seat) reveals Black, `side_assignment` locks RED→Black and BLACK→Red. Turn advances to the BLACK *seat*, who plays the Red *colour*. The sidebar shows the seat name ("Black 黑") when it should show the piece-colour-to-move ("Red 紅").

Fix: expose `current_color` on `PlayerView` (the seat-mapped piece colour) and have all "Turn:" labels use that.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-07 10:34:42Z)**_

Bundle rebuilt. Refresh the page to see the corrected label.

## What changed

- `PlayerView` gains a `current_color: Side` field (protocol v5, `serde(default = "default_red_side")` so v4 deserialisers still work). The server fills it from `state.current_color()` — i.e. the seat-mapped piece colour after a banqi first-flip locks `side_assignment`.
- All "Turn:" / "to move" labels now read `current_color`, not `side_to_move`:
  - `clients/chess-web/src/components/sidebar.rs` (local pass-and-play)
  - `clients/chess-web/src/pages/play.rs` (online sidebar)
  - `clients/chess-tui/src/ui.rs` (both local and net sidebars, two call sites)
- Seat-routing checks (`v.observer != v.side_to_move`, "is it MY turn?") still use `side_to_move` (the seat) — those need to compare seats, not piece colours.

## Expected behaviour now

Starting state: **Turn: Red 紅** (RED seat = Player 1 about to flip).

Player 1 flips a Black 車 → `side_assignment` locks RED→Black, BLACK→Red → turn advances to BLACK seat (= Player 2) → `current_color = Red` → sidebar reads **Turn: Red 紅**. Player 2's turn, playing the Red pieces — exactly what you described.

For xiangqi (no `side_assignment`) and banqi pre-first-flip, `current_color == side_to_move`, so the label is identical to before. All 251 workspace tests still pass.

---

```bash
git -C /Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(banqi): dark-capture, horse-diagonal, engine-driven 連吃 chain mode (protocol v5)

- 暗吃 (DARK_CAPTURE, renamed from DARK_CHAIN; bit unchanged) atomically
  reveals + captures a face-down piece. Three outcomes resolved at apply
  time: Capture (rank ok), Probe (rank fail, attacker stays), or Trade
  (rank fail + DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE flag → attacker dies). New
  Move::DarkCapture variant; chariot-rush also emits it across a gap.
- 馬斜 (HORSE_DIAGONAL) wired: 4 diagonal one-step moves; diagonal
  captures ignore rank.
- side_assignment is finally consulted by move-gen via
  GameState::current_color() — banqi now correctly remaps the
  seat-to-piece-colour after the first flip (was previously vestigial).
- Engine-driven 連吃 chain mode: state.chain_lock + Move::EndChain. After
  any chain-eligible capture in banqi+CHAIN_CAPTURE the turn does NOT
  auto-advance; legal_moves filters to "captures from locked square +
  EndChain" and the player ends the chain explicitly. Probe / Trade
  outcomes of DarkCapture correctly skip the lock (the attacker didn't
  land at `to`).
- PlayerView gains chain_lock + current_color (serde-default for v4
  back-compat). All "Turn:" labels in chess-tui + chess-web now display
  current_color (the piece-colour to move) instead of side_to_move (the
  seat name) so banqi's seat/colour divergence reads naturally to the
  player.
- chess-web: SVG board renders the chain-locked piece with a dashed
  orange ring; a "連吃 — 繼續吃 or End chain" banner sits below the board
  while chain mode is active. Picker exposes 暗吃 / 暗吃·搏命 / 馬斜
  checkboxes; URL tokens dark / dark-trade / horse (+ dark-chain alias).
- chess-tui: Esc / Enter on the locked piece sends Move::EndChain;
  sidebar uses current_color.
- ICCS notation: DarkCapture → `<from>x?<to>`, EndChain → `end <sq>`.
- 16 new engine tests (banqi_first_flip_color, banqi_dark_capture,
  banqi_horse_diagonal, banqi_chain_mode) cover make/unmake round-trips
  for every outcome including the perpendicular-capture-in-chain
  scenario the user reported (相 east → 砲 → 卒 south).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```
